feed.title: Elsa Riachi
feed.subtitle: A minimal, portfolio, sidebar, bootstrap Jekyll theme with responsive web design and focuses on text presentation.

entry.title: How Adversarial Attacks Exploit Shortcut Learning

link.title: How Adversarial Attacks Exploit Shortcut Learning

entry.summary: Current notions of non-robust features emphasize their presence in the data in some form. For instance … considers the reliance of neural networks on texture at the expense of shape, and … considers the entanglement of non-robust features with robust features. Other work conjectures that high frequency content in images is to blame for adversarial vulnerability. While these findings reveal impo...

entry.title: Constrained Optimization, The KKT conditions
entry.summary:
We examine how training a multi-class perceptron on adversarial attacks can lead to generalization. This exercise illustrates how adversarial attacks are well-generalizing without requiring the presence of non-robust features *in the dataset*.
$$ 
ewcommand	estmacro[2]{\mathbf{F\alpha}(#1)^{#2}} \def
orm#1{\left\|{#1}ight\|} % A norm with 1 argument 
ewcommand\zeronorm[1]{
orm{#1}_0}...

entry.title: Learning From Adversarial Examples (Part I)
entry.summary:
We examine how training a multi-class perceptron on adversarial attacks can lead to generalization. This exercise illustrates how adversarial attacks are well-generalizing without requiring the presence of non-robust features *in the dataset*.
$$ 
ewcommand	estmacro[2]{\mathbf{F\alpha}(#1)^{#2}} \def
orm#1{\left\|{#1}ight\|} % A norm with 1 argument 
ewcommand\zeronorm[1]{
orm{#1}_0}...

entry.title: The Structure of Adversarial Perturbations (Part I)

link.title: The Structure of Adversarial Perturbations (Part I)

entry.summary:
Adversarial vulnerability is a fundamental limitation of deep neural networks which remains poorly understood. Recent work suggests that adversarial attacks exploit the fact that non-robust models rely on superficial statistics to form predictions.
$$ 
ewcommand	estmacro[2]{\mathbf{F\alpha}(#1)^{#2}} \def
orm#1{\left\|{#1}ight\|} % A norm with 1 argument 
ewcommand\zeronorm[1]{
orm{#...
